entities.name: WorkflowPhaseSkillReference
entities.purpose: Names-first phase-local reference to an existing skill.
entities.notes: mode is recommended for this release. reason remains legacy-compatible data, not the applicability source.

entities.name: WorkflowSkillDependencyManifest
entities.purpose: Export-time manifest listing phase skill references and provenance without bundling skill contents.
entities.notes: Each dependency records templateId, phaseId, name, optional source/provenance, and export-time resolver status.

entities.name: WorkflowPhaseSkillResolution
entities.purpose: Resolver output shared by authoring, import/export diagnostics, runtime prompt assembly, and desktop status.
entities.notes: Status vocabulary must include available, missing, ambiguous, unsupported-source, plugin-disabled, invalid-reference, and optionally installable.

entities.name: WorkflowPhaseSkillSnapshot
entities.purpose: Session-start snapshot that freezes active phase skill interpretation for running/resumed/completed workflows.
entities.notes: Use snapshot data for runtime prompt/status even if source skill files later change.

entities.name: WorkflowPhaseSkillEvidence
entities.purpose: Durable, bounded evidence for used or clearly relevant skipped/unavailable recommended skills.
entities.notes: outcome should be used, relevant-skipped, or relevant-unavailable. Irrelevant recommendations are omitted.

state_transitions: created: store template references and optional import diagnostics; no runtime evidence yet.
running: resolve and snapshot active phase skills on start; prompt uses snapshot; late catalog changes are stale diagnostics, not silent semantic changes.
pending-confirmation: preserve phase skill snapshot and completion evidence; do not require recommended skill evidence.
failed/cancelled/completed: final report keeps signal-bearing skill evidence and resolution summary.
resumed: use persisted snapshot/evidence; project recovered state may project resumed status without recomputing skill meaning.
stale-template/missing-template: preserve snapshot and mark source-template status separately from skill resolution failure.
compatibility: Existing phase.skills arrays with { name, reason } remain valid and round-trip through unknown-field preservation.
Old exports without dependencyManifest remain importable.
Unknown fields on templates, phases, skills, artifacts, and final reports remain preserved.
